title: pelargonium × scottii
intro: Pelargonium × scottii, also known as the Scott's geranium or Barberton geranium, is a hybrid plant species within the family Geraniaceae, resulting from the cross between Pelargonium cordifolium and Pelargonium spp. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Pelargonium × scottii include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, relieve cold and flu symptoms, and soothe skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are commonly infused in hot water to make a tea, or used in tinctures and ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
